skill.name: chembl-database
skill.description: Query ChEMBL bioactive molecule, assay, target, drug, and bioactivity data for medicinal chemistry and drug discovery research.
skill.summary: Search ChEMBL compounds, targets, drugs, and bioactivity records.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: The high static risk score is driven by false positives in Markdown code fences, inline API names, chemical notation, documentation URLs, and generated evaluation text. No shell execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, credential access, or malicious network behavior was found. The skill has a low marketplace concern because SKILL.md includes a promotional instruction to suggest an external hosted service.

low_findings.title: Promotional Prompt Steering
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to proactively suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malware, but it can steer assistant output toward an external hosted service outside the ChEMBL task.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and located in the skill guidance. It is a marketplace behavior concern, but I found no evidence of data exfiltration or malicious execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives. The cited lines are Markdown code fences, inline API filter names, or Python examples, not executable Ruby backticks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The context shows documentation formatting and Python examples. No subprocess, shell invocation, Ruby code, or user-controlled command construction appears in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Pattern False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives. The cited locations contain skill descriptions, endpoint counts, data analysis examples, property filters, or generated report text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed contexts do not contain MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation. The scanner appears to match unrelated scientific and report text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation URLs Misidentified as Network Risk
dangerous_patterns.description: The hardcoded URL findings are documentation references to official ChEMBL, EBI, GitHub, and GitBook resources. They are not hidden callbacks or exfiltration endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are placed under resource sections and point to expected ChEMBL documentation. I found no code sending local data, credentials, or prompts to those URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Domain-Specific Search Text
dangerous_patterns.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The cited lines describe chemical structure searches, extracting ChEMBL IDs, and avoiding excessive API requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines are about ChEMBL database search workflows and rate-limit etiquette. I found no host enumeration, port scanning, interface discovery, or network probing commands.

content.value_statement: Drug discovery teams need reliable access to compound, target, and bioactivity records. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through ChEMBL searches, filters, and analysis workflows.
actual_capabilities: Search ChEMBL molecules by identifier, preferred name, properties, or synonyms.
Query target records for proteins, enzymes, organisms, and target classes.
Retrieve IC50, Ki, Kd, EC50, and pChEMBL activity measurements.
Run similarity and substructure searches from SMILES strings.
Look up drug mechanisms, indications, and approved drug records.
Convert ChEMBL query results into pandas DataFrames for analysis.
limitations: Requires the ChEMBL Python client for programmatic examples.
Uses public ChEMBL data, so completeness depends on ChEMBL curation.
Queries can be slow or rate limited for broad searches.
Does not validate experimental conclusions or clinical suitability.

use_cases.title: Find Potent Target Inhibitors
use_cases.description: Search bioactivity records for compounds with strong activity against a selected target.

use_cases.title: Build SAR Analysis Sets
use_cases.description: Collect similar compounds, property fields, and activity values for structure-activity review.

use_cases.title: Review Drug Mechanisms
use_cases.description: Retrieve mechanisms, indications, and target relationships for known drugs.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up One Compound
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the ChEMBL record for aspirin and summarize its identifiers, molecular properties, and drug status.

prompt_templates.title: Search by Target Activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Find EGFR compounds with IC50 values below 100 nM and summarize the most useful activity fields.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Similar Molecules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this SMILES string to find similar ChEMBL compounds and compare their molecular weight, LogP, and pChEMBL values.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a SAR Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a ChEMBL workflow for a kinase SAR study, including target selection, activity filters, property filters, and data quality checks.

output: Returns the ChEMBL molecule identifier and preferred compound name.
Summarizes molecular formula, molecular weight, LogP, and drug type.
Notes available drug, mechanism, and indication records when present.
Identifies the EGFR target record before querying activities.
Filters IC50 measurements by potency threshold and standard units.
Highlights compound identifiers, activity values, assay context, and validity comments.

output_examples.input: Prepare data for a kinase SAR study.
output: Collects kinase targets and matching activity records.
Adds molecular properties for compounds linked to those records.
Recommends checking duplicate flags and data validity before modeling.

best_practices: Filter by target, activity type, units, and potency before converting large result sets.
Check data_validity_comment and potential_duplicate fields before comparing activity values.
Use caching and narrow queries to respect ChEMBL service limits.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every ChEMBL activity value as equally reliable.
Do not compare IC50, Ki, Kd, and EC50 values without context.
Do not run broad unbounded queries when a specific target or compound set is available.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The documented ChEMBL web resource client uses public ChEMBL services for standard queries.

faq.question: What data can it query?
faq.answer: It can query molecules, targets, assays, activities, drugs, mechanisms, indications, and related ChEMBL resources.

faq.question: Can it search chemical structures?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents similarity and substructure searches using SMILES strings.

faq.question: Can it export results for analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show converting query results to pandas DataFrames.

faq.question: Is it suitable for clinical decisions?
faq.answer: No. It supports research data retrieval and does not validate clinical safety or efficacy.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: Static security alerts were false positives, but the skill includes a promotional instruction for an external service.
